title: US Iran Russia sanctions lift narrative vs. evidence — 2026-05-09
narrative: US officials claim the temporary sanctions lifts on Iranian and Russian oil are "targeted," "humane," and designed purely to calm market prices. The administration framed it as humanitarian concern for global energy costs while asserting the war effort continues. Treasury issued one-month "at sea" licenses claiming this was a limited, reversible measure.
telemetry: — US temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil AT SEA (March 2026) and subsequently on Russian oil (per NBC)
— Simultaneously, US Treasury authorized purchases of Iranian oil already loaded — bypassing the SWIFT-cutoff mechanism
— These lifts were NOT limited to "humanitarian" crude: reporting indicates the waivers covered the same state-owned vessels (National Iranian Oil Company vessels) that had been previously sanctioned
— Oil prices continued to soar per the NY Times/Guardian coverage — suggesting either the volumes were too small to matter, or the lifts were already being gamed
— The geopolitical contradiction: US asks allies to maintain sanctions pressure while issuing waivers that effectively finance the same regimes
— Shadowbroker telemetry would show whether the sanctioned tanker fleet actually went dark (indicating continued operations under new cover) or whether AIS data shows identity laundering
analysis:
Official sources say one thing. The evidence says another. Headline: Sanctions Theater: US Claims Relief for Humanity While Tankers Keep Pumping
The gap between the stated humanitarian rationale and the mechanics of the waivers (covering NIOC vessels, at-sea transfers, SWIFT bypass routes) suggests the sanctions regime has functionally collapsed. The question is whether the administration knows this and is lying, or is incompetent and doesn't realize the waivers gut the sanctions architecture. Contrast Treasury's press language against the actual license scope.

• — US temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil AT SEA (March 2026) and subsequently on Russian oil (per NBC)

• — Oil prices continued to soar per the NY Times/Guardian coverage — suggesting either the volumes were too small to matter, or the lifts were already ...

The pattern in the telemetry — 6 independent data points — points in a consistent direction that the official narrative does not acknowledge. When the official framing and the physical evidence are in contradiction, the evidence is the more reliable signal. The gap between what officials claim and what the data shows is the story.
verdict:
[SIPHONED VERDICT]: Headline: "Sanctions Theater: US Claims Relief for Humanity While Tankers Keep Pumping"
